_metadata.source_authority: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)
_metadata.governing_act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
_metadata.governing_rules: CGST Rules 2017 — Chapter VI (Rules 46–55A); CGST Act Sections 31–34; ICAI Handbook on Invoicing under GST (June 2025 edition)

rules.title: Tax Invoice for Supply of Goods — Time of Issue
rules.description: A registered person supplying taxable goods must issue a tax invoice before or at the time of removal of goods (where supply involves movement) or before or at the time of delivery or making available to recipient (where supply does not involve movement).
conditions: Before or at time of removal of goods, where supply involves movement of goods
Before or at time of delivery or making available to recipient, where supply does not involve movement
'Removal' means goods dispatched by supplier or collected by recipient
For exports: invoice must be issued before or at the time of removal since exports involve movement of goods
For continuous supply of goods: invoice at time of each statement of accounts or each payment, whichever is earlier
For goods sent on approval/sale-or-return: invoice at time when supply becomes known or 6 months from removal, whichever is earlier
rules.penalty_for_non_compliance: Section 122 of CGST Act — fine up to Rs. 10,000 or tax evaded, whichever is higher, per invoice; Section 132 for prosecution

rules.title: Tax Invoice for Supply of Services — Time of Issue
rules.description: A registered person supplying taxable services must issue a tax invoice before or after supply of service but within 30 days from date of supply (45 days for banking/insurance/NBFC).
conditions: General: within 30 days from date of supply of service
Banking companies, insurance companies, NBFCs: within 45 days from date of supply
Telecom operators or notified inter-company supplies between distinct persons: may issue invoice before or at time of recording in books, or before expiry of the quarter during which supply was made
For continuous supply of services: on or before the due date of payment as per contract, where payment is ascertainable; otherwise on or before each payment received
For cessation of contract before completion: at the time services cease, to the extent of supply made

rules.title: Self-Invoice by Recipient Under Reverse Charge
rules.description: Where a registered person receives supplies from an unregistered person and is liable to pay GST under RCM (Section 9(3) or 9(4)), the recipient shall issue a self-invoice within 30 days from the date of receipt of supply (Rule 47A, inserted w.e.f. 01.11.2024).
conditions: Applicable where supplier is unregistered and supply is covered under Section 9(3) or Section 9(4)
Self-invoice must be issued within 30 days from the date of receipt of goods or services
For registered supplier on Section 9(3) supplies: supplier issues invoice but must note 'tax payable on reverse charge'
Self-invoice follows same format as Rule 46 (all mandatory fields of a tax invoice)
Time of supply under RCM for goods is earlier of: date of receipt; date of payment in books; or 31 days from date of supplier's invoice/document
Self-invoice is the valid document for claiming ITC under Section 16 read with Rule 36(1)(b) for RCM supplies from unregistered persons
rules.official_source: https://cbic-gst.gov.in; ICAI Handbook on Invoicing under GST, June 2025

rules.title: Facility of Digital Payment to Recipient
rules.description: The Government may prescribe a class of registered persons who must provide modes of electronic payment to the recipient of supply of goods or services and give the recipient the option to make payment accordingly.
conditions: Applies to notified class of registered persons
The registered person must offer electronic payment modes as prescribed
Recipient must be given the option — it is not mandatory for the recipient to pay electronically

rules.title: Prohibition on Unauthorized Collection of Tax
rules.description: Only a registered person can collect tax on a supply in accordance with GST law. Any unauthorized collection of tax is prohibited.
conditions: An unregistered person cannot collect GST from recipients
A registered person cannot collect GST in excess of the rate specified under the Act
Charging GST on a nil-rated or exempt supply constitutes unauthorized collection
Tax collected in excess must be deposited with the Government

rules.title: Amount of Tax to Be Indicated in Tax Invoice
rules.description: The amount of tax charged on supply must be specifically indicated in the tax invoice in all cases. Where a registered person making a taxable supply does not charge tax, they are still required to issue a tax invoice or bill of supply.
conditions: Tax amount (CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess separately) must be explicitly shown on the invoice
The supplier cannot absorb or merge tax in the gross price without showing it separately on the invoice
Exception: where a tax invoice is not required to be issued (e.g., supplies less than Rs. 200 to unregistered persons in certain conditions)

rules.title: Credit Notes and Debit Notes
rules.description: Registered persons may issue credit notes where tax charged exceeds tax payable, or where goods are returned or deficient. Debit notes must be issued where tax charged is less than tax payable.
credit_note_conditions: Tax charged in the tax invoice exceeds the tax payable in respect of supply
Goods supplied are returned by the recipient
Goods or services supplied are found to be deficient
Credit note to be declared in the return for the month in which it was issued

Time limit for issuing credit note: no later than September 30 following the end of the financial year in which the supply was made OR the date of filing the relevant annual return, whichever is earlier
Credit note must contain the details prescribed under Rule 53 including original invoice number
debit_note_conditions: Tax charged in the tax invoice is less than the tax payable
Taxable value declared in invoice is less than actual value of supply
Debit note must be declared in the return for the month in which it was issued
No time limit prescribed for issuing debit notes (only credit notes have a time limit)

rules.title: Mandatory Particulars on a Tax Invoice
rules.description: Subject to Rule 54, a tax invoice under Section 31 must contain the following mandatory particulars.
mandatory_fields: (a) Name, address, and GSTIN of the supplier
(b) Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters, in one or multiple series, containing alphabets/numerals/special characters (hyphen, slash), unique for a financial year
(c) Date of issue of the invoice
(d) Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of the recipient if registered
(e) Name and address of recipient, delivery address, State name and State code if recipient is unregistered and taxable value >= Rs. 50,000
(f) HSN code of goods or SAC code of services as notified
(g) Description of goods or services
(h) Quantity (in case of goods) and unit/unique quantity code
(i) Total value of supply
(j) Taxable value taking into account discount or abatement
(k) Rate of tax — CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess
(l) Amount of tax charged — CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess
(m) Place of supply with State name, for inter-State supplies
(n) Address of delivery where different from place of supply
(o) Whether tax is payable on reverse charge basis
(p) Signature or digital signature of supplier or authorised representative
(q) QR code with embedded IRN, where invoice issued under Rule 48(4) (e-invoicing)
(r) Declaration that invoice is not required under Rule 48(4), where taxpayer's AATO exceeds notified threshold but is exempt from e-invoicing

requirements: AATO up to Rs. 5 Crore in preceding FY: 4-digit HSN on B2B invoices; optional on B2C
AATO above Rs. 5 Crore in preceding FY: 6-digit HSN on all invoices (B2B and B2C)

rules.invoice_below_rs200: A registered person may not issue a tax invoice if value of goods/services is less than Rs. 200 and the recipient is unregistered and does not require such invoice — a consolidated invoice may be issued at end of day for all such supplies
rules.penalty_for_non_compliance: Section 122 of CGST Act — up to Rs. 10,000 or tax evaded, whichever is higher, per invoice; Section 132 for prosecution

rules.title: Invoice-Cum-Bill of Supply
rules.description: Where a registered person makes a supply of both taxable and exempted goods/services to an unregistered person, a single document — invoice-cum-bill of supply — may be issued covering both taxable and exempt supplies.
conditions: Applicable only for supplies to unregistered persons
Must cover both a taxable supply (normally requiring a tax invoice) and an exempt supply (normally requiring a bill of supply)
Single document serves the purpose of both tax invoice and bill of supply
All mandatory fields of Rule 46 (for the taxable portion) must be present
All mandatory fields of Rule 49 (for the exempt portion) must be present

rules.title: Time Limit for Issuing Tax Invoice — Services
rules.description: The time limit for issuing a tax invoice for supply of taxable services.
conditions: General: within 30 days from the date of supply of service
Banking companies, insurance companies, NBFCs: within 45 days from date of supply

Telecom operators and other notified inter-company supplies (distinct persons under Section 25): may issue before or at time of recording in books or before expiry of quarter during which supply was made
Does NOT apply where recipient is required to issue the invoice (covered by Rule 47A)

rules.title: Time Limit for Invoice Where Recipient Issues (RCM Self-Invoice)
rules.description: Inserted w.e.f. 01.11.2024. Notwithstanding Rule 47, where invoice is required to be issued under Section 31(3)(f) by a registered person liable to pay tax under Section 9(3) or 9(4), the invoice must be issued within 30 days from date of receipt of supply.
conditions: Applies to registered persons receiving supplies attracting GST under RCM (Section 9(3) or 9(4))
30-day limit runs from date of receipt of goods or services
Overrides the general timelimit under Rule 47 for this scenario
Failure to issue within 30 days means the invoice is non-compliant and ITC may be denied

rules.title: Manner of Issuing Invoice — Physical Invoice (Non-E-Invoice)
rules.description: Invoices not covered by the e-invoice mandate under Rule 48(4) must be issued in the manner specified under Rule 48(1) and (2).
conditions: For goods: invoice to be prepared in triplicate — ORIGINAL FOR RECIPIENT, DUPLICATE FOR TRANSPORTER, TRIPLICATE FOR SUPPLIER
For services: invoice to be prepared in duplicate — ORIGINAL FOR RECIPIENT, DUPLICATE FOR SUPPLIER
Each copy must be clearly marked with the designation
Invoice may be issued in physical or electronic format (PDF, email etc.) provided the content complies with Rule 46

rules.title: Manner of Issuing Invoice — E-Invoice via IRP
rules.description: Notified registered persons must upload invoice particulars to the IRP in FORM GST INV-01 and obtain an IRN before issuing the invoice. Any invoice issued without a valid IRN by a notified person is not a valid invoice under Rule 48(5).
conditions: Applicable to registered persons with AATO exceeding the notified threshold (currently Rs. 5 Crore as of 01-08-2023 per Notification No. 10/2023-CT)
Invoice data must be uploaded to IRP in FORM GST INV-01 JSON format
IRP validates the data and issues a digitally signed JSON, IRN (64-character SHA-256 hash), and QR code
IRN and QR code must be embedded/printed on the invoice issued to the recipient
Invoice without valid IRN from notified taxpayer is deemed invalid — recipient loses ITC eligibility
From 01-04-2025: invoices must be reported to IRP within 30 days of issue (for taxpayers with AATO >= Rs. 10 Crore) per GSTN Advisory dated 05-11-2024
Cancellation of IRN: must be done within 24 hours on IRP; after 24 hours, amendment required in GSTR-1

rules.title: Exemptions from E-Invoice Mandate
rules.description: The following categories of registered persons are exempt from the e-invoice requirement under Rule 48(4) even where their AATO exceeds the notified threshold.
exempt_categories: Banking companies and financial institutions including NBFCs
Suppliers of services by way of admission to exhibition of cinematograph films on multiplex screens
Special Economic Zone (SEZ) Units (Note: SEZ Developers are NOT exempt)
Government departments and local authorities (Notification No. 23/2021-CT)
Persons registered under Rule 14 of CGST Rules (OIDAR service providers)

rules.title: Bill of Supply
rules.description: A registered person supplying exempt goods/services or paying tax under composition scheme shall issue a bill of supply instead of a tax invoice.
mandatory_fields: Name, address and GSTIN of the supplier
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Date of issue
Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of recipient (if registered)
HSN code of goods or accounting code for services
Description of goods or services
Value of supply after taking into account discount or abatement
Signature or digital signature of supplier or authorised representative
key_conditions: Tax rate and amount are NOT shown (since there is no tax on exempt supply or composition dealer supplies)
Composition dealer must mention 'composition taxable person, not eligible to collect tax on supplies' at the top of the bill of supply
Bill of supply is not required for supplies below Rs. 200 to unregistered persons unless requested
rules.applicable_to: Composition dealers; suppliers of exempt goods/services

rules.title: Receipt Voucher
rules.description: Where a registered person receives advance payment for any future supply, a receipt voucher must be issued at the time of receiving the advance.
mandatory_fields: Name, address and GSTIN of the supplier
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Date of issue
Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of recipient (if registered)
Description of goods or services
Amount of advance taken
Rate of tax (CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess)
Amount of tax charged
Place of supply with State name and code for inter-State supply
Whether tax is payable on reverse charge basis
Signature or digital signature of supplier or authorised representative
key_conditions: Mandatory for all advance receipts for supply of services — GST is payable on advance received for services
NOT applicable for advance for supply of goods (GST not payable on advance for goods per Notification No. 66/2017-CT)
If tax rate or nature of supply (goods/services) is not determinable, rate of 18% is used for receipt voucher
A tax invoice must be issued on actual supply and receipt voucher must be reconciled
If no supply is made after advance receipt, a Refund Voucher (Rule 51) must be issued

rules.title: Refund Voucher
rules.description: Where a registered person has issued a receipt voucher for an advance but subsequently no supply is made and no tax invoice is issued, a refund voucher must be issued to refund the advance.
mandatory_fields: Name, address and GSTIN of the supplier
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Date of issue
Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of the recipient
Serial number and date of corresponding receipt voucher
Description of goods or services for which advance was received
Amount of refund made
Rate of tax (CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess)
Amount of tax refunded
Place of supply with State name and State code for inter-State supply
Signature or digital signature of supplier or authorised representative
key_conditions: Must be issued when advance is refunded and supply has not been made
Must reference the original receipt voucher number and date
GST refunded through refund voucher can be adjusted in GSTR-1 return

rules.title: Payment Voucher (Reverse Charge)
rules.description: A registered person who is liable to pay tax under reverse charge mechanism (Section 9(3) or 9(4)) must issue a payment voucher at the time of making payment to the supplier.
mandatory_fields: Name, address and GSTIN of the supplier (if registered) / details of unregistered supplier
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Date of issue
Name, address and GSTIN of the recipient (i.e., the person issuing the voucher, who is the registered person paying tax under RCM)
Description of goods or services
Amount paid
Rate of tax (CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess)
Amount of tax payable
Place of supply with State name and State code
Signature or digital signature
key_conditions: Issued by the recipient (buyer) who is paying GST under RCM
Issued at the time of making payment to the supplier
If no payment is made (e.g., goods received but no payment yet), a Self-Invoice under Section 31(3)(f) is issued within 30 days of receipt (Rule 47A)

rules.title: Revised Tax Invoice and Credit or Debit Notes — Mandatory Fields
rules.description: Revised tax invoices (issued by newly registered persons for supplies made before registration) and credit/debit notes must contain the prescribed mandatory particulars under Rule 53.
revised_invoice_conditions: A revised tax invoice may be issued by a registered person against invoices already issued for the period between effective date of registration and grant of registration
Must be issued within 1 month from date of issuance of certificate of registration
Consolidated revised invoices may be issued for all taxable supplies to an unregistered recipient within a State/UT
mandatory_fields_credit_debit_note: Name, address and GSTIN of the supplier
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of the recipient
Serial number and date of the corresponding original tax invoice / bill of supply
Taxable value of goods or services, rate of tax, and the amount of tax credited or debited
rules.credit_note_time_limit: Must be issued no later than: September 30 following the end of the financial year in which the original supply was made, OR the date of filing the relevant annual return — whichever is earlier

rules.title: Tax Invoice — Banking Companies, NBFCs, Insurance (Special Cases)
rules.description: Banking companies, financial institutions, and NBFCs may issue any document in lieu of a tax invoice — the document need not contain all the fields prescribed under Rule 46.
conditions: Any document issued by a banking company, financial institution, or NBFC in lieu of a tax invoice is acceptable
The document must contain the name and address of the recipient and the amount of consideration
Such documents are valid for claiming ITC by recipients under Rule 36(1)(c)

rules.title: Tax Invoice — Goods Transport Agency (GTA)
rules.description: A Goods Transport Agency (GTA) must issue a tax invoice or a consignment note (in lieu of invoice) for its services.
mandatory_fields_consignment_note: Registration number of goods carriage in which goods are transported
Details of goods transported (description, quantity, value)
Person liable for paying tax — consigner, consignee, or GTA
key_conditions: If GTA is registered under forward charge (opted): issues a GST-compliant tax invoice
If GTA is under reverse charge: recipient pays GST; GTA issues a consignment note
From FY 2022-23: GTAs may opt to pay GST under forward charge (6% or 12%) instead of being covered under RCM

rules.title: Tax Invoice — Passenger Transport Services
rules.description: A registered person supplying passenger transport services may issue a ticket or any other document as a tax invoice — the ticket may serve as the invoice.
conditions: The ticket may be issued without containing the name and address of the recipient
GSTIN of recipient is not required on a passenger transport ticket
All other details — route, date, rate of tax, tax amount — must be present
Applicable to airlines, bus operators, railways (private), cab/taxi aggregators under GST

rules.title: Tax Invoice — Multiplex / Cinematograph Film Admission
rules.description: A registered person supplying services by way of admission to exhibition of cinematograph films on multiplex or other screens may issue a ticket as a tax invoice.
conditions: The ticket may be the invoice for GST purposes
Ticket must contain GST amount or GST-inclusive price indication
rules.applicable_to: Multiplex operators and cinematograph film exhibitors

rules.title: Tax Invoice — ISD (Input Service Distributor)
rules.description: An Input Service Distributor (ISD) must issue an ISD invoice or an ISD credit note for distributing input tax credit to its recipient units.
mandatory_fields: Name, address and GSTIN of the ISD
Consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters
Date of issue
Name, address and GSTIN of the recipient to whom credit is distributed
Amount of credit distributed
key_conditions: ISD must distribute ITC in the same month in which it is received (Rule 39(1)(a))
Eligible and ineligible ITC must be distributed separately (Rule 39(1)(b))
IGST, CGST, SGST/UTGST must be distributed separately
ISD cannot distribute more credit than it has received
From 01-04-2025: New ISD mechanism under amended Section 2(61) and Section 20 — ISD can also distribute ITC on inter-branch transactions (Finance Act 2024 amendment)

rules.title: Delivery Challan — Transportation Without Invoice
rules.description: Where goods are transported for reasons other than supply, a delivery challan must be issued. A tax invoice is issued only when supply is concluded.
applicable_situations: Supply of liquid gas where quantity at time of removal is not known
Transportation of goods for job work (inputs or capital goods sent to job worker)
Transportation of goods for reasons other than by way of supply (e.g., exhibition, loan, lease without transfer of title)
Transportation of goods in semi-knocked down (SKD) or completely knocked down (CKD) condition or in batches/lots
Other supplies as notified by the Board
mandatory_fields: Date and number of delivery challan
Name, address and GSTIN of consigner
Name, address and GSTIN/UIN of consignee (if registered)
HSN code, description, quantity and unit of goods
Taxable value
Tax rate and amount (CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess) — if taxable
Place of supply with State name (for inter-State movement)
Signature or digital signature
key_conditions: E-way bill to be generated on the basis of delivery challan if consignment value exceeds Rs. 50,000
Invoice to be issued after completion of supply/job work
For job work inputs: must return within 1 year; for capital goods: within 3 years

rules.title: Tax Invoice or Bill of Supply to Accompany Transport of Goods
rules.description: The person in charge of the conveyance must carry a copy of the tax invoice or bill of supply where an e-way bill is not required.
conditions: Applies where the transporter/person-in-charge is not required to carry an e-way bill
Must carry a copy of: Tax Invoice (Rule 46), Invoice-cum-Bill of Supply (Rule 46A), or Bill of Supply (Rule 49)
Ensures traceability of goods in transit

rules.rule_code: CGST Act Section 31 + Rule 46 — Export Invoice
rules.title: Invoice for Export of Goods or Services
rules.description: Invoices for export supplies (zero-rated supplies) must contain additional mandatory fields and endorsements.
mandatory_fields_exports: Endorsement: 'SUPPLY MEANT FOR EXPORT/SUPPLY TO SEZ UNIT OR SEZ DEVELOPER FOR AUTHORISED OPERATIONS ON PAYMENT OF INTEGRATED TAX' — for exports with IGST payment

Endorsement: 'SUPPLY MEANT FOR EXPORT/SUPPLY TO SEZ UNIT OR SEZ DEVELOPER FOR AUTHORISED OPERATIONS UNDER BOND OR LETTER OF UNDERTAKING WITHOUT PAYMENT OF INTEGRATED TAX' — for exports without IGST (LUT/Bond)
Name and address of recipient outside India
Shipping bill details (may be added later)
key_conditions: IGST-exempt exports (under LUT/Bond): IGST rate shown as 0 or nil; endorsement must state 'without payment of IGST'
IGST-paid exports: IGST charged at applicable rate; refund may be claimed
Export invoices in foreign currency: both foreign currency amount and INR equivalent must be shown
Invoice serial number may be in a separate series for exports (e.g., EXP/2024-25/001)

rules.rule_code: CGST Act Section 31 + Rule 46 — SEZ Invoice
rules.title: Invoice for Supplies to SEZ Units or SEZ Developers
rules.description: Supplies to Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units or developers are zero-rated supplies and invoices must carry the appropriate endorsement.
conditions: Supply to SEZ unit or developer for authorised operations: treated as zero-rated supply under Section 16 of IGST Act
Invoice must carry endorsement: 'SUPPLY TO SEZ UNIT OR SEZ DEVELOPER FOR AUTHORISED OPERATIONS'
Supplier may supply under bond/LUT (without IGST) or with IGST (and claim refund)
SEZ unit's certificate of operation and list of authorised operations should be on file with supplier
GSTIN of SEZ unit is a mandatory field on invoice

rules.title: Penalties for Non-Compliant Invoices
rules.description: Penalties and consequences for non-compliant invoices under the CGST Act.

penalty_provisions.offence: Supplying goods/services without issuing invoice, or issuing incorrect/false invoice
penalty_provisions.penalty: Rs. 10,000 or amount of tax evaded, whichever is higher — per invoice

penalty_provisions.offence: Issuing invoice/document in violation of Act/Rules
Incorrectly claiming ITC using false/fraudulent invoice
penalty_provisions.penalty: Rs. 10,000 or tax evaded/ITC wrongly claimed, whichever is higher

penalty_provisions.offence: Issuing invoice without actual supply OR fraudulently availing ITC using such invoice — if exceeds Rs. 5 Crore
penalty_provisions.penalty: Imprisonment up to 5 years with fine; non-bailable

penalty_provisions.offence: Invoice issued without valid IRN by a notified e-invoice taxpayer
penalty_provisions.penalty: Invoice treated as invalid; recipient loses ITC; penalty under Section 122
